titleStmt.title: Letter from J. C. Thompson to members of the Longford Urban District Council, 5 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. C. Thompson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Longford Nov 5th 15
HW Masses Gentlemen, I met Mr. J.J Fogarty here in
Longford on last Tuesday by appointment. I
went over all the defects that I require remedied,
and when all was finished he told me, point
blank, that he had no intention of doing any
more work. I received the enclosed communication
from him on Friday. yesterday. I cannot grant final certificate until he
remedies the defects existing and which I forwarded
to him more than once. J.J. Fogarty informs me that he purchased
his brother's estate from the Courts and is thus
entitled to recover all debts due to
Christopher Fogarty. Your obdt. Servant J.C. Thompson To. Longford Urban Dist Council
